lede: A look at February 28 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Vitor Roque and Carlos Dunlap, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: United States
events.desc: The United States and Israel launch attacks across Iran, assassinating Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. Retaliatory strikes are launched by Iran against US military bases in the Gulf, with explosions reported in Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar and the UAE.

events.title: Pope Benedict XVI
events.desc: Pope Benedict XVI resigns as the pope of the Catholic Church, becoming the first pope to do so voluntarily since Pope Gregory XII in 1415.

events.title: 2002 Gujarat violence
events.desc: During the religious violence in Gujarat, 97 people are killed in the Naroda Patiya massacre and 69 in the Gulbarg Society massacre.

events.title: 1997 Turkish military memorandum
events.desc: A Turkish military memorandum results in the collapse of the coalition government in Turkey.

events.title: Goodbye, Farewell and Amen
events.desc: The final episode of M*A*S*H airs, with almost 110 million viewers.

events.title: February 1974 United Kingdom general election
events.desc: The British election ends in a hung parliament after the Jeremy Thorpe-led Liberal Party achieved their biggest vote share since 1929.

events.title: Discoverer 1
events.desc: Discoverer 1, an American spy satellite that is the first object intended to achieve a polar orbit, is launched but fails to achieve orbit.

events.title: Protectorate
events.desc: The United Kingdom ends its protectorate over Egypt through a Unilateral Declaration of Independence.

events.title: National Covenant
events.desc: The Scottish National Covenant is signed in Edinburgh.

events.title: Emperor Gaozu of Han
events.desc: Liu Bang is enthroned as the Emperor of China, beginning four centuries of rule by the Han dynasty.

deaths.text: Charles Bassett, American captain, engineer, and astronaut

observances.text: Kalevala Day, also known as the Finnish Culture Day (Finland)
